Package space.sunqian.fs.object.pool

Interface SimplePool<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of objects in the pool
public interface SimplePool<T>
Simple object pool interface, provides methods for acquiring, releasing objects. If the pool is closed due to some exception, the unreleasedObjects() can be still invoked to get the list of unreleased objects.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Description
    static class 
    SimplePool.Builder<T>
    Builder class for SimplePool.

    activeSize()
    Returns the number of active objects in the pool.

    Acquires an object from the pool, or null if no object is available.

  • Method Details

    • newBuilder

      @Nonnull static <T> @Nonnull SimplePool.Builder<T> newBuilder()
      Returns a builder for SimplePool.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of objects in the pool
      Returns:
      a builder for SimplePool

    • get

      @Nullable T get() throws ObjectPoolException
      Acquires an object from the pool, or null if no object is available.

      If any exception occurs during the acquisition process, close() will be invoked to close this pool and the unreleasedObjects() will return the list of unreleased objects, including idle objects and active objects.

      Returns:
      the acquired object, or null if no object is available
      Throws:
      ObjectPoolException - if failed to acquire object

    • release

      boolean release(@Nonnull T obj) throws ObjectPoolException
      Releases the given object to the pool. Returns true if the object is released successfully, false otherwise. If the object is not acquired from this pool, this method will do nothing just return false.

      If any exception occurs during the release process, close() will be invoked to close this pool and the unreleasedObjects() will return the list of unreleased objects, including idle objects and active objects.

      Parameters:
      obj - the given object to release
      Returns:
      true if the object is released successfully, false otherwise
      Throws:
      ObjectPoolException - if failed to release object

    • clean

      void clean() throws ObjectPoolException
      Cleans the pool, removing idle objects that idle timeout or invalidated by validator, or over the core size, adding new objects up to the core size if necessary. The active objects will not be cleaned.

      If any exception occurs during the clean process, close() will be invoked to close this pool and the unreleasedObjects() will return the list of unreleased objects, including idle objects and active objects.

      Throws:
      ObjectPoolException - if any exception occurs during the clean process

    • close

      void close()
      Closes the pool. The idle objects will be discarded by configured discarder, and the active objects will not be discarded. If this process is failed, the pool will be in a closed state, and the unreleasedObjects() will return the list of unreleased objects, including idle objects and active objects.

    • isClosed

      boolean isClosed()
      Returns whether the pool is closed.
      Returns:
      true if the pool is closed, false otherwise

    • unreleasedObjects

      @Nonnull @Immutable @Nonnull @Immutable List<@Nonnull T> unreleasedObjects()
      Returns the list of unreleased objects after the pool is closed, including idle objects and active objects. If the pool is not closed, this method will return an empty list.
      Returns:
      the list of unreleased objects after the pool is closed, or an empty list if the pool is not closed

    • size

      int size()
      Returns the number of objects in the pool.
      Returns:
      the number of objects in the pool

    • idleSize

      int idleSize()
      Returns the number of idle objects in the pool.
      Returns:
      the number of idle objects in the pool

    • activeSize

      int activeSize()
      Returns the number of active objects in the pool. If the pool is closed, this method will return the number of unreleased active objects.
      Returns:
      the number of active objects in the pool
